10: from app.services.embeddings import EmbeddingService, EmbeddingError
11: from app.services.llm_client import LLMClient, LLMError
12: from app.services.memory_store import MemoryRecord, MemoryStore
13: from app.services.vector_store import VectorStore

22: @dataclass
23: class RAGSource:
24:     text: str
25:     file_id: str
26:     score: float
27:     metadata: Dict[str, Any]
28: 
29: 
30: class RAGEngineError(Exception):
31:     """Raised when the RAG engine cannot complete a query."""
32: 
33: 
34: class RAGEngine:
35:     """Coordinates embeddings, vector search, memory search, and LLM responses."""
36: 
37:     def __init__(
38:         self,
39:         embedding_service: Optional[EmbeddingService] = None,
40:         vector_store: Optional[VectorStore] = None,
41:         memory_store: Optional[MemoryStore] = None,
42:         llm_client: Optional[LLMClient] = None,
43:         reranking_service=None,  # NEW
44:     ) -> None:
45:         self.embedding_service = embedding_service or EmbeddingService()
46:         self.vector_store = vector_store or VectorStore()
47:         self.memory_store = memory_store or MemoryStore()
48:         self.llm_client = llm_client or LLMClient()
49:         self.reranking_service = reranking_service
50: 
51:         # Log warnings for missing dependencies (indicates non-DI usage)
52:         if embedding_service is None:
53:             logger.warning("RAGEngine created without injected embedding_service - using default instance")
54:         if vector_store is None:
55:             logger.warning("RAGEngine created without injected vector_store - using default instance")
56:         if memory_store is None:
57:             logger.warning("RAGEngine created without injected memory_store - using default instance")
58:         if llm_client is None:
59:             logger.warning("RAGEngine created without injected llm_client - using default instance")
60: 
61:         # Use new character-based fields, with fallback to legacy fields for backward compatibility
62:         self.chunk_size_chars = settings.chunk_size_chars
63:         self.chunk_overlap_chars = settings.chunk_overlap_chars
64:         self.retrieval_top_k = settings.retrieval_top_k
65:         self.vector_metric = settings.vector_metric
66:         self.max_distance_threshold = settings.max_distance_threshold
67:         self.embedding_doc_prefix = settings.embedding_doc_prefix
68:         self.embedding_query_prefix = settings.embedding_query_prefix
69:         self.retrieval_window = settings.retrieval_window
70: 
71:         # Reranking config
72:         self.reranking_enabled = settings.reranking_enabled
73:         self.reranker_top_n = settings.reranker_top_n
74:         self.initial_retrieval_top_k = settings.initial_retrieval_top_k
75: 
76:         # Hybrid search config
77:         self.hybrid_search_enabled = settings.hybrid_search_enabled
78:         self.hybrid_alpha = settings.hybrid_alpha
79: 
80:         # Legacy field support (deprecated) - warn if different from canonical fields
81:         self.relevance_threshold = settings.rag_relevance_threshold
82:         self.top_k = settings.vector_top_k
83:         if self.top_k is not None and self.top_k != self.retrieval_top_k:
84:             logger.warning(
85:                 "vector_top_k (%s) is deprecated and differs from retrieval_top_k (%s). "
86:                 "Using retrieval_top_k. Please update your settings.",
87:                 self.top_k, self.retrieval_top_k
88:             )
89:         self.maintenance_mode = settings.maintenance_mode
90: 
91:         # Query transformer instance (lazy-loaded)
92:         self._query_transformer: Optional[QueryTransformer] = None
93: 
94:         # Retrieval evaluator instance (lazy-loaded)
95:         self._retrieval_evaluator: Optional[RetrievalEvaluator] = None
96: 
97:     async def query(
98:         self,
99:         user_input: str,
100:         chat_history: List[Dict[str, Any]],
101:         stream: bool = False,
102:         vault_id: Optional[int] = None,
103:     ) -> AsyncIterator[Dict[str, Any]]:
104:         """Execute a RAG query: embed, search, build prompt, call LLM."""
105:         try:
106:             memory_content = self.memory_store.detect_memory_intent(user_input)
107:             if memory_content:
108:                 memory = self.memory_store.add_memory(memory_content, source="chat", vault_id=vault_id)
109:                 yield {
110:                     "type": "content",
111:                     "content": f"Memory stored: {memory.content}",
112:                 }
113:                 return
114:         except Exception as exc:
115:             logger.error("Memory intent detection/add failed: %s", exc)
116: 
117:         # Query transformation for broader retrieval (if enabled)
118:         transformed_queries: List[str] = [user_input]
119:         if settings.query_transformation_enabled and self.llm_client is not None:
120:             try:
121:                 if self._query_transformer is None:
122:                     self._query_transformer = QueryTransformer(self.llm_client)
123:                 transformed_queries = await self._query_transformer.transform(user_input)
124:                 if len(transformed_queries) > 1:
125:                     logger.info(
126:                         "Query transformation: original='%s', step_back='%s'",
127:                         transformed_queries[0],
128:                         transformed_queries[1]
129:                     )
130:             except Exception as e:
131:                 logger.warning("Query transformation failed: %s, using original query only", e)
132:                 transformed_queries = [user_input]
133: 
134:         # Embed all transformed queries
135:         query_embeddings: List[List[float]] = []
136:         for query_text in transformed_queries:
137:             try:
138:                 embedding = await self.embedding_service.embed_single(query_text)
139:                 query_embeddings.append(embedding)
140:             except EmbeddingError as exc:
141:                 logger.warning("Failed to embed query variant '%s': %s", query_text, exc)
142: 
143:         if not query_embeddings:
144:             error_msg = "Unable to encode any query variants"
145:             if stream:
146:                 yield {"type": "error", "message": error_msg, "code": "EMBEDDING_ERROR"}
147:                 return
148:             raise RAGEngineError(error_msg)
149: 
150:         fallback_reason: Optional[str] = None
151:         vector_results: List[Dict[str, Any]]
152:         relevance_hint: Optional[str] = None
153:         
154:         # DEBUG: Log pre-vector-search state
155:         logger.debug(
156:             "RAG query: retrieval_top_k=%d, vault_id=%s, vector_store_connected=%s",
157:             self.retrieval_top_k,
158:             vault_id,
159:             getattr(self.vector_store, 'is_connected', lambda: 'unknown')()
160:         )
161:         
162:         if self.maintenance_mode:
163:             fallback_reason = "RAG index is under maintenance"
164:             vector_results = []
165:         else:
166:             try:
167:                 # Stage 1: Initial retrieval
168:                 fetch_k = self.initial_retrieval_top_k if self.reranking_enabled else self.retrieval_top_k
169:                 top_k_value = fetch_k if fetch_k is not None else self.retrieval_top_k
170: 
171:                 # Search with all query embeddings and fuse results
172:                 all_results: List[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = []
173:                 for embedding in query_embeddings:
174:                     results = await asyncio.to_thread(
175:                         self.vector_store.search,
176:                         embedding,
177:                         int(top_k_value),
178:                         vault_id=str(vault_id) if vault_id is not None else None,
179:                         query_text=user_input if embedding is query_embeddings[0] else "",
180:                         hybrid=self.hybrid_search_enabled and embedding is query_embeddings[0],
181:                         hybrid_alpha=self.hybrid_alpha,
182:                     )
183:                     all_results.append(results)
184: 
185:                 # Fuse results from all query variants using RRF
186:                 if len(all_results) > 1:
187:                     vector_results = rrf_fuse(all_results, k=60, limit=fetch_k)
188:                     logger.info("Fused results from %d query variants: %d results", len(all_results), len(vector_results))
189:                 else:
190:                     vector_results = all_results[0] if all_results else []
191:                 
192:                 # Log vector search results
193:                 logger.info(
194:                     "Vector search: vault_id=%s, top_k=%d, results=%d, distances=%s",
195:                     vault_id,
196:                     top_k_value,
197:                     len(vector_results),
198:                     [r.get("_distance") for r in vector_results[:3]] if vector_results else "N/A"
199:                 )
200: 
201:                 # Filter by distance threshold
202:                 filtered_results = []
203:                 for record in vector_results:
204:                     distance = record.get("_distance")
205:                     if distance is not None and self.max_distance_threshold:
206:                         if distance > self.max_distance_threshold:
207:                             continue
208:                     filtered_results.append(record)
209:                 vector_results = filtered_results
210: 
211:                 # Stage 2: Reranking (if enabled)
212:                 if self.reranking_enabled and self.reranking_service and vector_results:
213:                     try:
214:                         vector_results = await self.reranking_service.rerank(
215:                             query=user_input,
216:                             chunks=vector_results,
217:                             top_n=self.reranker_top_n,
218:                         )
219:                     except Exception as e:
220:                         logger.warning(f"Reranking failed, using original results: {e}")
221: 
222:                 # Final limit to retrieval_top_k
223:                 vector_results = vector_results[:self.retrieval_top_k]
224: 
225:                 # Retrieval evaluation (CRAG-style self-evaluation)
226:                 relevance_hint = None
227:                 if settings.retrieval_evaluation_enabled and self.llm_client is not None:
228:                     try:
229:                         if self._retrieval_evaluator is None:
230:                             self._retrieval_evaluator = RetrievalEvaluator(self.llm_client)
231:                         eval_result = await self._retrieval_evaluator.evaluate(user_input, vector_results)
232:                         if eval_result == "NO_MATCH":
233:                             logger.info("Retrieval evaluation: NO_MATCH for query '%s'", user_input)
234:                             relevance_hint = "Note: The retrieved documents may not be directly relevant to your query."
235:                         elif eval_result == "AMBIGUOUS":
236:                             logger.warning("Retrieval evaluation: AMBIGUOUS for query '%s'", user_input)
237:                     except Exception as e:
238:                         logger.warning("Retrieval evaluation failed: %s", e)
239:             except Exception as exc:
240:                 fallback_reason = str(exc)
241:                 vector_results = []
242: 
243:         relevant_chunks = self._filter_relevant(vector_results)
244:         if fallback_reason:
245:             logger.warning("Vector search fallback triggered: %s", fallback_reason)
246:             yield {
247:                 "type": "fallback",
248:                 "reason": fallback_reason,
249:                 "results": [],
250:                 "total": 0,
251:                 "fallback": True,
252:             }
253:         try:
254:             memories = await asyncio.to_thread(
255:                 self.memory_store.search_memories,
256:                 user_input,
257:                 settings.max_context_chunks,
258:                 vault_id=vault_id,
259:             )
260:         except Exception as exc:
261:             logger.error("Memory search failed: %s", exc)
262:             memories = []
263: 
264:         messages = self._build_messages(user_input, chat_history, relevant_chunks, memories, relevance_hint)
265: 
266:         if stream:
267:             try:
268:                 async for chunk in self.llm_client.chat_completion_stream(messages):
269:                     yield {"type": "content", "content": chunk}
270:             except LLMError as exc:
271:                 yield {"type": "error", "message": str(exc), "code": "LLM_ERROR"}
272:                 return
273:         else:
274:             try:
275:                 content = await self.llm_client.chat_completion(messages)
276:             except LLMError as exc:
277:                 raise RAGEngineError(f"LLM chat failed: {exc}") from exc
278:             yield {"type": "content", "content": content}
279: 
280:         # Build retrieval debug info
281:         retrieval_debug: Dict[str, Any] = {
282:             "max_distance_threshold": self.max_distance_threshold,
283:             "vector_metric": self.vector_metric,
284:             "retrieval_top_k": self.retrieval_top_k,
285:         }
286:         
287:         yield {
288:             "type": "done",
289:             "sources": [self._source_metadata(c) for c in relevant_chunks],
290:             "memories_used": [mem.content for mem in memories],
291:             "retrieval_debug": retrieval_debug,
292:         }
293: 
294:     def _normalize_metadata(self, metadata: Any) -> Dict[str, Any]:
295:         """Ensure metadata is a dict, parsing JSON string if needed."""
296:         if isinstance(metadata, dict):
297:             return metadata
298:         if isinstance(metadata, str):
299:             try:
300:                 parsed = json.loads(metadata)
301:                 if isinstance(parsed, dict):
302:                     return parsed
303:             except (json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError) as exc:
304:                 logger.warning("Failed to parse metadata JSON: %s", exc)
305:                 pass
306:         return {}
307: 
308:     def _filter_relevant(self, results: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> List[RAGSource]:
309:         sources: List[RAGSource] = []
310:         distances: List[float] = []
311:         
312:         # DEBUG: Log pre-filtering state
313:         input_count = len(results)
314:         logger.debug(
315:             "Filtering: input_results=%d, max_distance_threshold=%s",
316:             input_count,
317:             self.max_distance_threshold
318:         )
319:         if results:
320:             first_distances = [r.get("_distance", r.get("score")) for r in results[:5]]
321:             logger.debug("First few _distance values: %s", first_distances)
322:         
323:         for record in results:
324:             # Use _distance from LanceDB (lower is better for cosine)
325:             has_distance = "_distance" in record
326:             distance = record.get("_distance")
327:             if distance is None:
328:                 score = record.get("score")
329:                 if score is None:
330:                     score = 1.0
331:                 distance = score
332:             
333:             distances.append(distance)
334:             
335:             # Use max_distance_threshold if set (new field)
336:             # Otherwise use relevance_threshold (legacy field) for backward compatibility
337:             threshold = self.max_distance_threshold
338:             if threshold is None:
339:                 threshold = self.relevance_threshold
340:             
341:             # Determine if we should skip this record based on threshold
342:             # For _distance (lower=better): skip if distance > threshold
343:             # For score (higher=better): skip if score < threshold
344:             should_skip = False
345:             if threshold is not None:
346:                 if has_distance:
347:                     # Using _distance (lower is better)
348:                     should_skip = distance > threshold
349:                 else:
350:                     # Using score (higher is better, for backward compatibility)
351:                     should_skip = distance < threshold
352:             
353:             if should_skip:
354:                 continue
355:                 
356:             sources.append(
357:                 RAGSource(
358:                     text=record.get("text", ""),
359:                     file_id=record.get("file_id", ""),
360:                     score=distance,  # Store distance (lower=better) or score (higher=better)
361:                     metadata=self._normalize_metadata(record.get("metadata")),
362:                 )
363:             )
364:         
365:         # Log retrieval stats
366:         if distances:
367:             initial_count = len(distances)
368:             filtered_count = len(sources)
369:             min_dist = min(distances)
370:             max_dist = max(distances)
371:             mean_dist = sum(distances) / len(distances)
372:             
373:             logger.info(
374:                 "Vector search: initial=%d, filtered=%d, min=%.3f, max=%.3f, mean=%.3f, threshold=%.3f",
375:                 initial_count, filtered_count, min_dist, max_dist, mean_dist, self.max_distance_threshold
376:             )
377:         
378:         # Apply window expansion if enabled
379:         if self.retrieval_window > 0:
380:             sources = self._expand_window(sources)
381:         
382:         # DEBUG: Log filtering results summary
383:         filtered_count = len(sources)
384:         if input_count > 0 and filtered_count == 0:
385:             logger.warning(
386:                 "Filtering issue: %d input results but 0 after filtering. "
387:                 "Check max_distance_threshold (%.3f) - distances may exceed threshold.",
388:                 input_count,
389:                 self.max_distance_threshold
390:             )
391:         logger.debug("Filtering complete: %d results returned", filtered_count)
392:         
393:         return sources
394:     
395:     def _expand_window(self, sources: List[RAGSource]) -> List[RAGSource]:
396:         """
397:         Expand search results by fetching adjacent chunks (N±window).
398:         
399:         Args:
400:             sources: Initial list of RAGSource chunks from vector search
401:             
402:         Returns:
403:             Expanded list of RAGSource chunks with adjacent context
404:         """
405:         if not sources:
406:             return sources
407:         
408:         window = self.retrieval_window
409:         
410:         # Group sources by (file_id, chunk_scale) to avoid cross-scale window mixing
411:         file_chunks: Dict[str, List[RAGSource]] = {}
412:         for source in sources:
413:             # Use (file_id, chunk_scale) as key to separate different scales
414:             chunk_scale = source.metadata.get("chunk_scale", "default")
415:             if chunk_scale == "default" or chunk_scale is None:
416:                 group_key = source.file_id
417:             else:
418:                 group_key = f"{source.file_id}_{chunk_scale}"
419:             
420:             if group_key not in file_chunks:
421:                 file_chunks[group_key] = []
422:             file_chunks[group_key].append(source)
423:         
424:         # Collect all chunk_uids to fetch (including adjacent chunks)
425:         chunk_uids_to_fetch: List[str] = []
426:         
427:         # Track chunk_count per file from the initial results
428:         file_chunk_indices: Dict[str, List[int]] = {}
429:         
430:         for group_key, file_sources in file_chunks.items():
431:             # Extract file_id and chunk_scale from group_key
432:             # group_key is either "file_id" (default) or "file_id_scale"
433:             if "_" in group_key:
434:                 parts = group_key.rsplit("_", 1)
435:                 if len(parts) == 2:
436:                     file_id, chunk_scale = parts
437:                 else:
438:                     file_id = group_key
439:                     chunk_scale = "default"
440:             else:
441:                 file_id = group_key
442:                 chunk_scale = "default"
443:             
444:             indices = [int(s.metadata.get("chunk_index", 0)) for s in file_sources]
445:             
446:             # Calculate adjacent indices for each chunk
447:             for chunk_index in indices:
448:                 # Calculate window range: [chunk_index - window, chunk_index + window]
449:                 start_idx = max(0, chunk_index - window)
450:                 end_idx = chunk_index + window
451:                 
452:                 # Generate UIDs for all indices in the window
453:                 # Handle both 2-part (file_id_idx) and 3-part (file_id_scale_idx) formats
454:                 for idx in range(start_idx, end_idx + 1):
455:                     if chunk_scale != "default":
456:                         chunk_uid = f"{file_id}_{chunk_scale}_{idx}"
457:                     else:
458:                         chunk_uid = f"{file_id}_{idx}"
459:                     chunk_uids_to_fetch.append(chunk_uid)
460:         
461:         # Fetch adjacent chunks from vector store
462:         if chunk_uids_to_fetch:
463:             adjacent_chunks = self.vector_store.get_chunks_by_uid(chunk_uids_to_fetch)
464:             
465:             # Create a lookup for adjacent chunks by their uid
466:             adjacent_lookup: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}
467:             for chunk in adjacent_chunks:
468:                 chunk_id = chunk.get("id", "")
469:                 if chunk_id:
470:                     adjacent_lookup[chunk_id] = chunk
471:             
472:             # Build expanded sources list
473:             expanded_sources: List[RAGSource] = []
474:             seen_uids: set = set()
475:             
476:             # First, add the original sources
477:             for source in sources:
478:                 chunk_index = source.metadata.get("chunk_index", 0)
479:                 # Handle both 2-part and 3-part UID formats based on chunk_scale
480:                 chunk_scale = source.metadata.get("chunk_scale", "default")
481:                 if chunk_scale and chunk_scale != "default":
482:                     uid = f"{source.file_id}_{chunk_scale}_{chunk_index}"
483:                 else:
484:                     uid = f"{source.file_id}_{chunk_index}"
485:                 if uid not in seen_uids:
486:                     expanded_sources.append(source)
487:                     seen_uids.add(uid)
488:             
489:             # Then, add adjacent chunks that aren't already in the results
490:             for chunk_uid in chunk_uids_to_fetch:
491:                 if chunk_uid in seen_uids:
492:                     continue
493:                 
494:                 # Parse uid to get file_id, chunk_scale (optional), and chunk_index
495:                 # Handle both 3-part (file_id_scale_index) and 2-part (file_id_index) formats
496:                 parts = chunk_uid.rsplit("_", 2)
497:                 
498:                 if len(parts) == 3:
499:                     # 3-part format: file_id_scale_index
500:                     file_id, chunk_scale, chunk_index_str = parts
501:                 elif len(parts) == 2:
502:                     # 2-part format: file_id_index
503:                     file_id, chunk_index_str = parts
504:                     chunk_scale = None
505:                 else:
506:                     continue
507:                 
508:                 try:
509:                     chunk_index = int(chunk_index_str)
510:                 except ValueError:
511:                     continue
512:                 
513:                 # Check if this chunk exists in adjacent_lookup
514:                 if chunk_uid in adjacent_lookup:
515:                     chunk = adjacent_lookup[chunk_uid]
516:                     
517:                     # Calculate score based on distance (lower is better for cosine)
518:                     has_distance = "_distance" in chunk
519:                     distance = chunk.get("_distance")
520:                     if distance is None:
521:                         score = chunk.get("score")
522:                         if score is None:
523:                             score = 1.0
524:                         distance = score
525:                     
526:                     # Get metadata and add chunk_scale if parsed from 3-part UID
527:                     metadata = self._normalize_metadata(chunk.get("metadata"))
528:                     if chunk_scale:
529:                         metadata["chunk_scale"] = chunk_scale
530:                     
531:                     expanded_source = RAGSource(
532:                         text=chunk.get("text", ""),
533:                         file_id=file_id,
534:                         score=distance,
535:                         metadata=metadata,
536:                     )
537:                     expanded_sources.append(expanded_source)
538:                     seen_uids.add(chunk_uid)
539:             
540:             # Sort by (file_id, chunk_index)
541:             def sort_key(source: RAGSource) -> tuple:
542:                 chunk_index = source.metadata.get("chunk_index", 0)
543:                 try:
544:                     chunk_index = int(chunk_index)
545:                 except (ValueError, TypeError):
546:                     chunk_index = 0
547:                 return (source.file_id, chunk_index)
548:             
549:             expanded_sources.sort(key=sort_key)
550:             
551:             # Cap to retrieval_top_k total
552:             if len(expanded_sources) > self.retrieval_top_k:
553:                 expanded_sources = expanded_sources[:self.retrieval_top_k]
554:             
555:             return expanded_sources
556:         
557:         # If no adjacent chunks fetched, return original sources
558:         return sources
559: 
560:     def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:
561:         return (
562:             "You are KnowledgeVault, a highly accurate assistant that references sources when "
563:             "answering questions. Cite the relevant documents or memories by name."
564:         )
565: 
566:     def _build_messages(
567:         self,
568:         user_input: str,
569:         chat_history: List[Dict[str, Any]],
570:         chunks: List[RAGSource],
571:         memories: List[MemoryRecord],
572:         relevance_hint: Optional[str] = None,
573:     ) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:
574:         context_sections = [self._format_chunk(ch) for ch in chunks]
575:         memory_context = [mem.content for mem in memories if mem.content]
576: 
577:         messages = [
578:             {"role": "system", "content": self._build_system_prompt()},
579:         ]
580:         for entry in chat_history:
581:             messages.append(entry)
582: 
583:         context = "\n\n".join(filter(None, context_sections))
584:         
585:         # DEBUG: Log context being sent to LLM
586:         logger.info(
587:             "Building messages: chunks=%d, memories=%d, context_length=%d",
588:             len(chunks),
589:             len(memory_context),
590:             len(context)
591:         )
592:         if context:
593:             logger.debug("Context preview (first 500 chars): %s", context[:500])
594:         
595:         user_content_parts = []
596:         if relevance_hint:
597:             user_content_parts.append(relevance_hint)
598:         if context:
599:             user_content_parts.append(f"Context:\n{context}")
600:         else:
601:             user_content_parts.append("No relevant documents found for this query.")
602:         user_content = "\n\n".join(user_content_parts) + "\n\n"
603: 
604:         memory_text = "\n".join(memory_context)
605:         if memory_text:
606:             user_content += f"Memories:\n{memory_text}\n\n"
607: 
608:         user_content += f"Question: {user_input}"
609:         messages.append({"role": "user", "content": user_content})
610:         return messages
611: 
612:     def _format_chunk(self, chunk: RAGSource) -> str:
613:         source_title = chunk.metadata.get("source_file") or chunk.metadata.get("section_title") or "document"
614:         return f"Source {source_title} (score: {chunk.score:.2f}):\n{chunk.text}"
615: 
616:     def _source_metadata(self, chunk: RAGSource) -> Dict[str, Any]:
617:         filename = (
618:             chunk.metadata.get("source_file")
619:             or chunk.metadata.get("filename")
620:             or chunk.metadata.get("section_title")
621:             or "Unknown document"
622:         )
623:         return {
624:             "id": chunk.file_id,
625:             "file_id": chunk.file_id,
626:             "filename": filename,
627:             "snippet": chunk.text[:300] if chunk.text else "",
628:             "score": chunk.score,
629:             "metadata": chunk.metadata,
630:         }
